objective.content_objective: Students will be able to identify ways Lenni Lenape descendants preserve their culture today and explain effects of European colonization on the Lenape community.
objective.student_goal: I will explain how Lenni Lenape preserve culture (reading, writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain how Lenni Lenape descendants preserve culture and describe colonization effects (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Reading/Writing) by using preview-review and cognate supports appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing a short biography slide that demonstrates use of evidence and organized sentences.

anticipatory_set.original_content: In your own words define the word indigenous. Next create a complete sentence (capital letter, subject, predicate and punctuation) that uses the word indigenous. Teachers can use chart paper and post it notes, padlet or another format to record and display student responses.
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Activate Portuguese cognates: display the 6 cognate pairs and ask students in Portuguese what they notice about similarities. 2) Provide a simple Portuguese prompt: "O que significa indígena?" and have students write a one-sentence Portuguese response before writing in English.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Core vocabulary for identifying Lenni Lenape and discussing culture.
Central concept for discussing preservation practices.
Key verb used when describing actions to keep traditions alive.
Used to name practices that descendants maintain.
Vocabulary for identifying individuals (e.g., Curtis Zunigha).
Academic term for cultural inheritance referenced in the text.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Key vocabulary terms and definitions can be provided to students ahead of time in order to better comprehend the text as they read independently.
Discussing, clarifying, and breaking down the language of the learning targets to ensure comprehension
Present questions for students to see on the board as they are discussed with the class
Chunking the text into smaller sections for deeper analysis
Ask students to periodically self-assess their own learning to give an idea of how much they accomplished over the course of the lesson

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant inference with mixed beginner support needs; station teaching allows targeted L1 preview and scaffolded practice in small rotations.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: WIDA Proficiency Context: Mixed range with typical grade 3 distribution; several students needing word/phrase supports and others ready for sentence-level tasks.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead Portuguese cognate activation and quick paired translation warm-up; model one example.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Review learning targets and post questions for whole class.
phase_plan.details: Use chart paper and post-its; display the 6 cognate pairs.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Work at Station A with 4-6 students: preview vocabulary in Portuguese, use the 6 cognate pairs, and guide annotation.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Conduct whole-group read-aloud and monitor comprehension at other stations.
phase_plan.details: Chunk Chapter 9 into short sections for stations.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support biography slide authorship at Station B: model sentence frames, scaffold evidence selection, and check grammar in both languages.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Circulate to provide content feedback and support writing mechanics.
phase_plan.details: Rotations: Station A (vocab/portuguese preview), Station B (writing support), Station C (independent reading).

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a quick share-out using one sentence frame in English and one in Portuguese.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect exit tickets and confirm next steps.
phase_plan.details: Use a visual timer for rotations and a shared chart for evidence points.

implementation_notes: Prepare 6 cognate cards and sentence frame strips before class.
Use a visual timer and rotation chart for smooth transitions.
warnings: If planning time is less than 45 minutes, fidelity of stations may be reduced; prioritize a two-station model.

ell_support.implementation: Use the 6 cognate pairs for a matching activity and quick Portuguese-to-English comparisons at Station A. Teach students how cognates can help infer meaning; refer generically to "the 6 cognate pairs" rather than listing them during instruction.
Provide level-banded frame strips (levels 1-2, 3-4, 5-6) during writing and speaking stations; prompt students to use at least one frame when composing biography slide.
Use a simple organizer to collect evidence from text/video; bilingual teacher models filling organizer in Portuguese then in English with students following.

special_needs_support: Provide sentence starters and highlighted text for students with reading IEP goals.
Allow drawing responses or oral recordings for students with writing accommodations.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may not have a deep contextual understanding.

linguistic_note.note: Students may overgeneralize translation word-for-word and miss English sentence structure differences.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model parallel sentences in Portuguese and English highlighting subject-verb order and provide guided practice with frames.

assessment.primary_assessment:
Daily Instructional Task: Biography
Students will choose one Lenape descendant of the indigenous people of North America from the lesson (Curtis Zunigha, Desmond Medera, or Beedohskah Stonefish) and complete a biography slide.
Students will re-read the text or re-watch the video about that individual.
Use the[ Biography Slide Template](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1xBSBfJRc4O0i-pa-UHpZa6L-wRzcTqHHjhNKEVa_mmE/edit?usp=sharing) to complete the following:
Provide basic information about the person.
Describe how they are working to keep Lenape culture alive today.
Use specific details and evidence from the text or video.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Biography slide (informational/explanatory product with evidence citations).

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide word/phrase banks (from the 6 cognate pairs) and allow drawing with a one-sentence caption in English; L1 preview and Portuguese sentence modeling.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and a bilingual graphic organizer to record evidence; allow short bilingual notes then full English sentences.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Encourage extended English sentences and use of connectors; offer reduction of supports to promote independent writing.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score content accuracy and evidence use (3 pts), organization and sentence completeness (2 pts), language use for targeted ELD level (2 pts).
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials required; uses Biography Slide Template and existing text/video as specified.

homework.family_connection: Family interview: In Portuguese, ask a family member about a tradition they keep and write one English sentence about it in the reading log.

objective.content_objective: Students will learn that previewing a text activates prior knowledge and that characters change throughout a story.
objective.student_goal: I will preview and read to find character change (reading, writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain how a character changes across a story (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Reading/Writing) by using previewing and sentence frames and cognate support appropriate for WIDA levels 1-3, producing a written sentence and an oral explanation that demonstrates comprehension and use of targeted vocabulary.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Refer to the second grade ELA curriculum learning guide: 02 - Second Grade Unit Description and Summary of Key Learning (https://docs.google.com/document/d/1H_hFmN04OarxjXNlCsHxSgkzyD1nfzMjDFWQfx5zc30/edit?usp=sharing)
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Teacher previews the book cover and title in English and asks students in Portuguese what they think the story might be about to activate background knowledge. 2) Teacher highlights the words "character" and "change" in English, provides the Portuguese equivalents orally, and connects to students' experiences of how people change over time.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Central term for discussing who the story is about and how they change.
Core text type students will read and discuss.
Key concept when tracking how a character develops.
Strategy students use before reading to activate prior knowledge.
Skill used during preview to anticipate events in the story.
Related to the assessment task where students set and work toward a reading goal.

tailored_instruction.original_content: Stories read aloud to a small group. Small group instruction with ESL and Bilingual Teachers. Supporting resources provided. Sentence stems.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Default class profile inferred as Levels 3-4 dominant; Station Teaching allows targeted small-group bilingual preview and practice during a 45-minute session.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant typical second grade distribution inferred; mixed emergent readers present.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a brief bilingual cover-preview and model prediction in Portuguese then English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Introduce the text and set the reading goal for the group.
phase_plan.details: Use the 6 cognate pairs to introduce vocabulary quickly.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Pre-teach key vocabulary and sentence frames in Portuguese and English for the station group.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Read aloud and model thinking aloud for another station group.
phase_plan.details: Visuals and word cards referenced from primary materials.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Facilitate guided practice with sentence frames and cognate matching; circulate between students to prompt use of frames.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Oversee partner reading station and monitor fluency practices.
phase_plan.details: Rotation: three stations of ~7 minutes each with visual timers.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Elicit one oral sentence from students using target frames; reinforce the meaning in Portuguese briefly.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect reading goals and quick exit evidence of understanding.
phase_plan.details: Quick share and sticky-note reflection.

implementation_notes: Prepare three small stations with clear visual roles and a rotation chart.
Use a visual timer and simple rotation cards; bilingual teacher brings vocabulary cards with Portuguese glosses.

ell_support.implementation: Introduce the 6 cognate pairs during warmup; use a matching activity at the bilingual station where students pair English words with Portuguese equivalents and explain meaning using sentence frames. Refer to these items as "the 6 cognate pairs." Do not list the pairs in this implementation text.
Model each frame in both languages and provide sentence strips; during practice prompt students to complete frames orally and in writing, scaffolding with word banks derived from the 6 cognate pairs.

Before reading, bilingual teacher previews key images and vocabulary in Portuguese and English; after reading, review main idea and character change in Portuguese to consolidate understanding and transfer to English.

special_needs_support: Provide sentence starters with visual icons for students needing support.
Allow oral response instead of written response for students with writing IEP goals.
materials: Big book or shared text provided by primary teacher
Index cards with the 6 cognate pairs and sentence frame strips

misconceptions.original_content: Students may think they can only read at their desk or in the library. Students may not understand what the word independent means. Students may not be able to work independently.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese often drops subject pronouns, so students may omit English subject pronouns when speaking or writing.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model full English sentences repeatedly and provide sentence frames that include the subject; practice choral repetition and have students underline the subject in sentences.

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will be assessed based on the reading goal they set for themselves. This goal will allow the teacher to determine the students' understanding of what a goal means and their ability to write in a complete sentence.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Student-set reading goal and accompanying written sentence + short oral explanation.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide a picture prompt and sentence starter; allow students to state their goal orally in Portuguese then produce a short English phrase with support.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and a 3-word word bank including 2 of the 6 cognate pairs; encourage a full sentence in English with one detail.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Require a complete English sentence with evidence of character change or preview strategy; encourage added detail and self-assessment.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Focus on ability to state a clear reading goal (intention), use of at least one target vocabulary word, and production of a complete sentence (subject + verb + detail) appropriate to the student's level.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials required beyond primary teacher resources; supports use only paper/pencil and word bank cards already referenced in lesson.

homework.original_content: Read for 20 minutes and log. Spelling (ABC Order).
homework.family_connection: At home, families can ask the child in Portuguese to preview the book cover and tell one thing they predict; child records the prediction in English in their reading log.

slots.subject: Math
slots.unit_lesson: Unit 4 Lesson 1: Whole numbers on the Number Line

objective.content_objective: Students will understand the structure of the number line and represent whole numbers with tick marks and points.
objective.student_goal: I will place numbers and points on a number line (reading, speaking).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform representing whole numbers on a number line (ELD-MA.2-3.Inform.Reading/Writing) by using cognate awareness and sentence frames appropriate for Levels 1-4, producing labeled number lines and short oral explanations that demonstrate understanding.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Unit 4: Addition and Subtraction on the Number Line
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Show the Portuguese word for 'number' and 'linha' and ask students to point to matching visuals. 2) Invite one student to say the number location in Portuguese then in English before starting the activity.

Defines the space where numbers are placed.
Students use tick marks to locate whole numbers.
Represents the exact place of a number on the line.
Used when students judge where an unlabeled number sits.
Needed when students explain which number is greater on the line.

tailored_instruction.original_content: Small group instruction, Manipulatives (counting cubes), numberlines, Visuals

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant class benefits from differentiated stations where bilingual teacher provides targeted language scaffolds while primary teacher rotates for content support.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Typical 2nd grade distribution inferred as Levels 3-4 dominant with some Levels 1-2 and occasional Level 5 students; station model allows targeted support.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Preview 2 Portuguese-English vocabulary words and model one sentence frame with students.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Quick whole-group activation and demonstration with a large number line poster.
phase_plan.details: Use visuals to connect Portuguese terms to the number line.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Work with a small group to pre-teach vocabulary and practice sentence frames in Portuguese then English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Demonstrate tick mark placement and show counting strategy to whole class.
phase_plan.details: Use counting cubes to show spacing between tick marks.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Run a station with 4 students focusing on guided practice using sentence frames and manipulatives; provide Portuguese prompts as needed.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Run alternate station focused on independent practice and assessment observation.
phase_plan.details: Stations: (1) Bilingual teacher station, (2) Practice with counting cubes, (3) Partner talk using frames.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Facilitate a short share-out using one target sentence frame; reinforce vocabulary in Portuguese then English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect quick exit tickets and summarize key points.
phase_plan.details: Use visual timer and rotation chart for smooth transitions.

implementation_notes: Prepare 3 station mats: bilingual support, manipulative practice, partner talk with sentence frames.
Use visual timers and number cards to manage 5-minute mini-rotations during practice.
Bilingual teacher brings a portable poster with the 6 cognate pairs and sentence frame strips.

ell_support.implementation: Introduce the 6 cognate pairs at the start station and use matching cards in the bilingual station. Students match English word cards to Portuguese cards, say each pair aloud, and then use the English word in a simple sentence frame. Refer to them as the 6 cognate pairs; do not re-list them here.

Provide color-coded frame strips for each proficiency band. At the bilingual station model frames in Portuguese, then support students to produce the English frame with a partner. Use choral and echo reading for Levels 1-2.

Use counting cubes and pocket number lines with Portuguese labels for initial practice. Bilingual teacher demonstrates how to map cubes to tick marks and uses gestures to reinforce left/right language.

special_needs_support: Provide a tactile number line strip and allow finger tracing for students with fine motor needs.
Allow extended time for the cool-down exit ticket and reduce item count if needed.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may struggle with understanding why groups are getting different answers. Remind students how we use classroom math tools.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese often omits subject pronouns; students may say phrases lacking subjects when speaking English, which can make explanations unclear.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model full English sentences during frame practice and prompt students to use the subject (I/You/He) when they explain positions; provide a subject word bank on the frame strip.

assessment.primary_assessment: 2.3.1 Cool Down

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Short written cool-down exit ticket: place three numbers on a small number line and write one sentence explaining your choice.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide a word bank with the 6 cognate pairs, allow drawing of points instead of writing full sentences, and permit Portuguese planning notes.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence stems from the sentence_frames set and allow bilingual whisper support from the bilingual teacher during station rotations.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect short English explanation; offer optional Portuguese-to-English checklist for planning; encourage use of comparative language (left/right).

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score using the primary rubric for correct placement (content) and add a language lens: Levels 1-2: word/phrase labeling and accurate point placement; Levels 3-4: use of a complete sentence stem showing position and reasoning; Levels 5-6: coherent explanation with comparative language.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; uses counting cubes, number lines, visuals already referenced by the teacher.

homework.family_connection: At home, ask a family member to draw a simple number line and have your child place 3 numbers, saying the positions aloud in Portuguese and English (e.g., 'o número está à direita' then 'the number is to the right').

objective.content_objective: Students will comprehend that "nearest multiple of 100" means the multiple of 100 closest to a given number (written and spoken).
objective.student_goal: I will explain rounding and write an estimate (speaking, writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain how to identify the nearest multiple of 100 (ELD-MA.2-3.Explain.Reading/Writing) by using sentence frames and number-line visuals appropriate for WIDA levels 2-4, producing short written estimates and oral explanations that demonstrate understanding of nearest multiples.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Warm-up: Estimation Exploration: Marching Band (10 minutes)
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) In Portuguese, elicit a quick example: ask students "Qual número está mais próximo de cem?" and have a volunteer answer. 2) Show a short number-line labeled in Portuguese and English to connect the L1 term "cem" with the English "hundred" before returning to the English activity.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Core unit for rounding to the nearest 100.
Needed to name multiples of 100 and explain closeness.
Key comparative term when deciding which multiple is closest.
Action verb students use when estimating to a multiple.
Describes the product students create after rounding.
Visual tool used to place two- and three-digit numbers.

tailored_instruction.original_content: Activity 1: About 100? Close to 100? (10 minutes). Activity 2: Close to Multiples of 100 (25 minutes). Students locate two- and three-digit numbers on a series of number lines and discuss closeness to multiples of 100.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant (typical grade 3 distribution) benefit from small-group stations where the bilingual teacher pre-teaches key vocabulary and supports transfer.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Mixed WIDA proficiency with majority at Levels 3-4 and some at Levels 2 and 5; Stations allow targeted scaffolds.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead bilingual quick Portuguese-English vocabulary connection and model a sample rounding using a labeled number line.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate whole-class estimation prompt and set norms.
phase_plan.details: Use a quick choral count to orient to tens and hundreds; bilingual teacher models one example in Portuguese then English.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Work with Station A (emerging students) using Portuguese scaffolds and manipulatives to show nearest multiple.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Teach Station B (developing students) with guided practice on number lines in English.
phase_plan.details: Rotate students after 10 minutes; visual anchor charts remain at each station.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Circulate and co-teach with small groups, offering sentence-frame prompts in Portuguese then English and clarifying misconceptions for Levels 1-3.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Observe student strategies, confer with groups, and support reasoning tasks for Levels 3-5.
phase_plan.details: Stations: (A) Bilingual guided rounding, (B) Partner number-line placement, (C) Independent quick checks.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Elicit a volunteer explanation using sentence frames; reinforce Portuguese-English vocabulary connections.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Select a student to present reasoning and provide final formative feedback.
phase_plan.details: Use exit question from cool-down as quick check.

implementation_notes: Prepare three station signs and one bilingual number-line anchor chart prior to lesson.
Use visual timers for 10/25 minute rotations and clear rotation chart.
Bilingual teacher brings small set of index cards with the 6 vocabulary pairs for Station A.

ell_support.implementation: Introduce the 6-word bilingual list at the start of the lesson; at Station A the bilingual teacher sorts cards into 'look/sound alike' and 'different' piles, and students match English words to Portuguese words before practice.
Provide labeled number-line strips with both English and Portuguese labels; bilingual teacher models placing a number and saying the nearest multiple in both languages.
Use the three Levels 3-4 frames during partner talk; bilingual teacher rehearses the frames in Portuguese then prompts students to try in English.

special_needs_support: Allow students to point to number-line anchors instead of writing answers when fine motor is a barrier.
Provide a small-group verbal check-in with scaffolded choices (A/B/C) for students needing reduced language output.
materials: index cards with the 6 vocabulary pairs
visual anchor chart showing multiples of 100

misconceptions.original_content: Students may struggle and confuse rounding to the nearest 100 versus nearest 10.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese often omits subject pronouns; students may produce English phrases like "Is closest to 100" without a subject.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model full English sentences using frames ("This number is closest to 100 because ___") and have students repeat with the subject aloud before writing.

assessment.primary_assessment: [3.3.13 Cool-down](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1VgP93ihY9fpAoIVc-CQRRUJyk9HFCuqN/view?usp=drive_link)

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Short written cool-down: identify nearest multiple of 100 for three numbers (written response) plus one oral justification.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide a mini number-line and word bank in Portuguese-English; allow point-and-say responses or picture-supported choices.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames in English + Portuguese translations and a two-term word bank (hundred, multiple).
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Ask for a short written justification using the stem: "I rounded to ___ because ___." Encourage full sentences.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Score for mathematical accuracy (nearest multiple identified) and for language: Levels 1-2 credit phrase/word identification; Levels 3-4 credit a sentence using a frame; Levels 5-6 credit a clear explanation with cause ('because').
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No new materials introduced; uses existing cool-down instrument and classroom number-line strips.

homework.family_connection: With a caregiver, pick three prices from a grocery flyer and ask in Portuguese: "Qual número está mais próximo de cem?" Then student explains in English which multiple is closest and why (short sentence).

objective.content_objective: Students will describe properties of different materials (color, shape, mass, texture).
objective.student_goal: I will describe material properties with words and pictures (reading, writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform properties of materials (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Reading/Writing) by using cognate awareness and sentence frames appropriate for WIDA levels 1-3, producing labeled diagrams and short sentences that demonstrate vocabulary and descriptive skill.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Refer to 2nd grade science curriculum starting with page 126
2nd Grade Science
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1. Activate L1 background: Teacher shows a familiar household object and says its name in Portuguese, then English. 2. Link to concept: Ask in Portuguese "Que propriedades você vê?" then prompt students in English to label one property.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Core term for discussing what materials are made of and their properties.
Key concept when observing that shape change does not change how much matter is present.
Useful when comparing how much space an object takes up.
Students will describe and compare object shapes during activities.
Material state that helps children categorize objects.
Sensory property students will observe and record using manipulatives.

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Differentiated Instruction
Small Groups
Visuals
Manipulatives

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant assumption for 2nd grade class; station teaching allows targeted scaffolds and hands-on practice.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with a mix of emerging Level 1-2 students and some Level 4 students.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model vocabulary in Portuguese and English; quick vocabulary matching with visuals.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Introduce the objective and demonstrate one example.
phase_plan.details: Show one example object and label 2 properties in Portuguese and English; students point and repeat.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a station focused on cognate awareness and sentence frames (L1 preview, guided practice, check-ins).
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Rotate through stations teaching content and monitoring science investigations.
phase_plan.details: Three stations: (1) Bilingual vocabulary & observation station, (2) Manipulatives for testing mass/shape, (3) Drawing and recording station. Use a 10-minute rotation schedule.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Facilitate quick share-out in pairs using sentence frames.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect notebooks and highlight one example from student work.
phase_plan.details: Two quick paired share-outs; select one pair to report to whole class.

implementation_notes: Prepare 6 vocabulary cards (English front, Portuguese back) and a visual anchor chart.
Use a visual timer and rotation chart for smooth station shifts.

ell_support.implementation: Teacher introduces the 6 cognate pairs in English and Portuguese, highlighting similar spellings and meanings. Use matching card sorts and a quick partner quiz. Refer to the 6 cognate pairs below when prompting students. Do not re-list the pairs here; use them in station activities and as word banks on anchor charts.

Provide printed frame strips at the bilingual station and model oral completion in Portuguese then English. Use frames from the sentence_frames list and encourage students to use one frame during share-out.
Use a simple T-chart showing 'What I see' and 'What I notice about texture/mass' with labels in English and Portuguese. Bilingual teacher pre-teaches how to fill the chart during station time.

special_needs_support: Provide picture cues and simplified sentence frames for students with language needs.
Allow extra time and use manipulatives for students with fine motor or processing delays.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may confuse what properties are.

linguistic_note.note: Students may translate 'properties' literally and confuse it with ownership phrasing in Portuguese.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Pre-teach the meaning with visuals and L1 examples; use the T-chart to separate 'what it looks like' vs 'what it does'.

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will complete p. 46 in their Be a Scientist Notebook

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Notebook page observation and short written labels; teacher circulates and records oral responses.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide word/phrase banks with picture cues in Portuguese and English; allow oral response recorded by teacher.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and model responses; allow bilingual notes and labelled drawings.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Encourage full-sentence explanations using frames and compare/contrast vocabulary; reduce scaffolds as appropriate.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Focus on use of target vocabulary, ability to match property to object, and use of sentence frames at each level (phrase vs sentence).
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses only Be a Scientist Notebook and classroom materials referenced by the primary teacher; no additional handouts required.

homework.family_connection: At home, families can point to three household items and ask in Portuguese/English: 'What is its shape? What is its texture?' Students draw one item and label one property in both languages.

slots.subject: ELA
slots.unit_lesson: Unit 3: [Lenni Lenape](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_yHvguDhJIZmrIwY3ZcW-BIhfQH5VtH2xs1KtKlnNVY/edit?tab=t.9vfaqefj1p25) - Lesson 11: Delaware Lenape Chapters 10 and 11

objective.content_objective: Students will identify connections between Lenni Lenape history and present preservation efforts and notice text details that inform these connections.
objective.student_goal: I will describe how the past connects to the present (reading, writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain connections between Lenni Lenape history and present preservation efforts (ELD-LA.2-3.Explain.Reading/Writing) by using graphic organizers and sentence frames with bilingual supports appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing a short explanatory paragraph that demonstrates use of textual evidence.

anticipatory_set.original_content:
Remind students of prior learning, “As we have learned throughout this unit, the Lenape community has been cut down due to war and disease. Due to the decrease in their population they have been forced to migrate.”
Explain the focus for today: “You will work in groups to read the next few chapters to identify ways in which the Lenape people work to preserve their culture.”
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) In Portuguese, briefly retell one prior example of preservation discussed earlier. 2) Preview three key vocabulary words in Portuguese and English and ask students to predict where those words might appear in the chapter.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Explains movement of Lenni Lenape populations referenced in the chapters.
Term for citing textual proof when explaining preservation efforts.
Used when describing efforts to maintain traditions.
Key for discussing group actions and projects.
Refers to practices maintained by the Lenape.
Describes outreach efforts to teach the public about history and culture.

sentence_frames.english: The people moved because of ___ .
sentence_frames.portuguese: As pessoas se mudaram por causa de ___ .

sentence_frames.english: I found evidence that ___ in the text ___ .
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu encontrei evidência de que ___ no texto ___ .

sentence_frames.english: They educate others by ___ .
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eles educam os outros por ___ .

sentence_frames.english: This example shows ___ because the author wrote ___ .
sentence_frames.portuguese: Este exemplo mostra ___ porque o autor escreveu ___ .

sentence_frames.english: The community responds to past events by ___ which leads to ___ .
sentence_frames.portuguese: A comunidade responde a eventos passados por ___ que leva a ___ .

sentence_frames.english: I can use evidence such as ___ to support that ___ .
sentence_frames.portuguese: Posso usar evidências como ___ para apoiar que ___ .

sentence_frames.english: Evidence from the text suggests that the community preserves traditions by ___ which indicates ___ .
sentence_frames.portuguese: Evidências do texto sugerem que a comunidade preserva tradições por ___ o que indica ___ .

sentence_frames.english: How does an action like educating the public help preserve heritage?
sentence_frames.portuguese: Como uma ação como educar o público ajuda a preservar a herança?

tailored_instruction.original_content:
Key vocabulary terms and definitions can be provided to students ahead of time in order to better comprehend the text as they read independently.
Discussing, clarifying, and breaking down the language of the learning targets to ensure comprehension.
Present questions for students to see on the board as they are discussed with the class.
Chunking the text into smaller sections for deeper analysis
Ask students to periodically self-assess their own learning to give an idea of how much they accomplished over the course of the lesson.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Stations prioritized to allow Portuguese previews and small-group evidence practice; suited to mixed proficiency with some newcomers.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: WIDA Proficiency Context: Mixed but with several students requiring phrase-level support.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Preview key vocabulary in Portuguese and model how to locate evidence.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Frame the group reading focus and post guiding questions.
phase_plan.details: Use the note catcher and chart paper.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead small groups to read Chapters 10-11 and complete graphic organizers with Portuguese support as needed.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate other groups and check comprehension across stations.
phase_plan.details: Students rotate every 8-10 minutes between stations.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support group reporting: model one full sentence in English using a frame, then prompt students to share.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect organizers and provide feedback for writing task.
phase_plan.details: Use a class carousel to gather evidence points.

implementation_notes: Bring bilingual graphic organizers and highlight sentence frames for each level.
Assign clear roles within groups to increase participation.

ell_support.implementation: Use the Culture Preservation Graphic Organizer to capture textual evidence; bilingual teacher models filling one organizer in Portuguese then English. Students use organizer to plan explanatory writing.
Brief mini-lesson on the 6 cognate pairs; students work in pairs to find cognates in the chapter and mark them in Portuguese or English.
Use sentence frames to structure small-group talk before writing; bilingual teacher models in Portuguese then supports translation into English.

special_needs_support: Provide guided notes and pre-highlighted text for students with decoding difficulties.
Allow oral response recorded on device for students with fine motor challenges.

misconceptions.original_content:
Students may not have a deep contextual understanding.
Students may struggle to identify how the Lenni people keep their traditions alive.

linguistic_note.note: Students may assume direct one-to-one word translations convey full meaning; idiomatic or cultural meanings may be missed.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model conceptual definitions in Portuguese and English and use examples from the text showing usage.

assessment.primary_assessment:
Daily Instructional Task: Informational/Explanatory Writing
Students will use their[ Culture Preservation Graphic Organizer](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1OrLBdZHu8PTQa9rkrKdpcwNnPvtRW555QF0FiNffulc/edit?usp=sharing) to collect evidence from the text.
Students will explain why the Lenape work hard to keep their culture alive.
Include evidence from the text to support their answer.
Use complete sentences and precise language, and clear organization.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Short explanatory paragraph using collected evidence (note catcher as scaffold).

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Use word banks and sentence starters; allow bilingual brainstorming before writing English sentences.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and graphic organizer to plan and draft a paragraph.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Require two pieces of evidence and a concluding statement; encourage use of discourse connectors.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Evaluate evidence use (3 pts), organization (2 pts), language appropriate to level (2 pts).
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses existing note catcher and class materials; no additional handouts needed.

homework.family_connection: Family conversation: In Portuguese, ask an older family member to recall a family tradition and write one sentence in English describing how it is preserved.

objective.content_objective: Students will learn editing and publishing steps and practice fluency when reading aloud.
objective.student_goal: I will edit and publish my writing (reading, writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform how they edit and publish a short piece of writing (ELD-LA.2-3.Inform.Reading/Writing) by using editing checklists, sentence frames, and bilingual prompts appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing a revised draft and a short published sentence.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Refer to the second grade ELA curriculum learning guide: 02 - Second Grade Unit Description and Summary of Key Learning (https://docs.google.com/document/d/1H_hFmN04OarxjXNlCsHxSgkzyD1nfzMjDFWQfx5zc30/edit?usp=sharing)
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Start with a shared example of a "before" and "after" sentence in English; ask students in Portuguese which is clearer and why. 2) Provide Portuguese labels for editing steps (check capitals, punctuation, spacing) and link these to the English checklist.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Central process for improving student writing.
Final stage of the writing process being practiced.
Unit of writing students will edit and publish.
Editing convention students must check.
Students will check punctuation during editing.
Students will revise drafts toward publication.

sentence_frames.english: I will fix this sentence: ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu vou corrigir esta sentença: ___

sentence_frames.english: My draft needs ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Meu rascunho precisa de ___

sentence_frames.english: I will make a better sentence by ___
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu vou fazer uma frase melhor por ___

sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu editei minha sentença mudando ___ para ___ porque ___

sentence_frames.portuguese: Para publicar, eu vou checar maiúsculas e pontuação como ___

sentence_frames.english: What change in the draft most improved the meaning and why?
sentence_frames.portuguese: Qual mudança no rascunho mais melhorou o significado e por quê?

tailored_instruction.original_content: Students will be provided assistance with fluent reading to help them practice on their own. Small group instruction with ESL and Bilingual Teachers.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant inference; Parallel Teaching lets bilingual and primary teacher lead similar instruction to reduce group size and increase opportunities for practice.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Inferred Levels 3-4 dominant; groups organized to scaffold editing and fluency practice.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Quick review of editing checklist in Portuguese and English with examples.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model fluent reading and editing example with one student.
phase_plan.details: Use a shared checklist visible to all groups.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model sentence-level edits and provide guided practice in a small group.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Teach parallel group focusing on fluency and publishing steps.
phase_plan.details: Both teachers use the same checklist language to maintain consistency.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Confer with small groups to support editing, prompting Portuguese where needed.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Circulate and support students practicing fluent reading for publication.
phase_plan.details: Students rotate through peer-editing and fluency stations.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Confirm publishing steps in Portuguese and collect one published sentence example.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Have students share a published sentence and celebrate improvements.
phase_plan.details: Quick reflection and reminder for homework.

implementation_notes: Use bilingual checklists and color-coded editing marks.
Prepare a simple publishing rubric that both teachers reference.

ell_support.implementation: Highlight common differences between Portuguese and English punctuation/capitalization and provide mini-lessons and visual reminders; use the 6 cognate pairs as part of editing word banks.
Pair stronger bilingual readers with peers to support fluency and editing using sentence frames and the editing checklist.
Provide frames for editing explanations and model how to use them when conferencing; students use frames to explain edits in English with Portuguese scaffolds as needed.

special_needs_support: Provide scribe support or keyboard options for students with fine-motor difficulties.
Allow extra time for editing tasks and reduce required sentence length for students with processing needs.
materials: Sentence frame strips and word bank cards

misconceptions.original_content: Students may not understand how to use decoding strategies without prompting. Students may not be able to read independently.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese adjective placement differs from English, which can affect student editing choices (e.g., adjective after noun in Portuguese).
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Teach explicit examples showing noun + adjective order in English and provide comparison charts during editing.

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will be monitored while they are shopping for books, the teacher will assess them based off their ability to independently do this task. In addition to their independence while shopping for books, the teacher will monitor their ability to read independently.

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Observation of independent book selection and reading fluency; teacher notes on editing/publishing steps observed.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide visual choice boards for book selection and scripted sentence frames for explaining choices in Portuguese/English.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide a short checklist and sentence frames to explain selection and reading fluency.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Ask students to explain their publishing choices in a full sentence and provide one example of an edit they made.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Observe independence in selecting an appropriate text, use of editing checklist language, and explanation of a revision using a sentence frame.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: No additional materials beyond classroom resources; overlay uses observation and existing checklists.

homework.original_content: Read for 20 minutes and log. Spelling (color code-constants and vowels).
homework.family_connection: Families can help students practice reading a short paragraph aloud and point out one capitalization or punctuation change in Portuguese, asking the child to explain the change in English.

slots.subject: Math
slots.unit_lesson: Unit 4 Lesson 2: Features of a number line

objective.content_objective: Students will identify and describe defining features of the number line.
objective.student_goal: I will name number line parts and explain (reading, speaking).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Inform identifying features of a number line (ELD-MA.2-3.Inform.Reading/Writing) by using picture labels and sentence frames, appropriate for Levels 1-4, producing labeled diagrams and short oral descriptions.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Unit 4: Addition and Subtraction on the Number Line
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Display a labeled number line with Portuguese feature labels (número, marca, ponto). 2) Invite students to point and name each feature first in Portuguese, then in English.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Essential for naming tick marks and points.
Feature students must recognize and label.
Helps students describe spacing between tick marks.
Used to mark exact values on the line.
Action of writing numbers at tick marks.
Direction vocabulary for comparing positions.

sentence_frames.english: This part is the ___ (tick mark/point).
sentence_frames.portuguese: Esta parte é a ___ (marca/ponto).

sentence_frames.english: The interval between ___ and ___ is ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: O intervalo entre ___ e ___ é ___.

sentence_frames.english: I label the tick mark with ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu rotulo a marca com ___.

sentence_frames.english: The interval from ___ to ___ shows ___ because ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: O intervalo de ___ a ___ mostra ___ porque ___.

sentence_frames.english: I can label this point as ___ and justify it by ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu posso rotular este ponto como ___ e justificá-lo por ___.

sentence_frames.english: This tick mark is to the left of ___ so ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Esta marca está à esquerda de ___ então ___.

sentence_frames.english: Describe how interval size affects placement of numbers and give an example ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Descreva como o tamanho do intervalo afeta a colocação dos números e dê um exemplo ___.

sentence_frames.english: How would you relabel this number line to show halves or quarters?
sentence_frames.portuguese: Como você rotularia esta linha numérica para mostrar meios ou quartos?

tailored_instruction.original_content: Small group instruction, Manipulatives (counting cubes), numberlines, Visuals

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant distribution supports stations where bilingual teacher focuses on vocabulary and labeling while primary teacher runs applied practice.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Class likely includes emerging bilinguals; station teaching allows targeted L1 support and scaffolded input.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Quick vocabulary preview with picture labels in Portuguese and English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model labeling on a projected number line.
phase_plan.details: Use matching cards for interval and tick mark.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Demonstrate labeling and use of the word bank; elicit student repetition in both languages.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Show sample student work and highlight correct labels.
phase_plan.details: Point to left/right and label in both languages.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a station where students label mini number lines using the 6 cognate pairs and sentence frames.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Circulate between stations, checking conceptual understanding.
phase_plan.details: Use pre-cut number strips for students who need fine motor support.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Facilitate partner share using a frame and encourage use of Portuguese-English labels.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect cool-down responses and provide quick feedback.
phase_plan.details: Use a two-sentence rubric for labeling and explanation.

implementation_notes: Pre-cut number lines for students who need help with cutting.
Place picture-word cards at the bilingual station to support decoding.
Use a visual checklist for correct labeling steps.

ell_support.implementation: Provide laminated picture-word cards with English and Portuguese labels for number line features; students match cards to parts, then verbalize the match using a sentence frame.
Bilingual teacher writes labels with students on a shared chart first in Portuguese then model translation to English; students copy into their mini number lines.
Use choral repetition for simple frames (Levels 1-2) and partner practice for Levels 3-4; cue students with gestures for left/right.

special_needs_support: Provide pre-glued number strips for students needing fine motor assistance.
Allow a scribe for students who cannot write but can orally explain their labeling.

misconceptions.original_content: Students may need materials cut for them. Students may not know how to properly use math materials to assist their learning.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese speakers may produce verbs without explicit subjects, making English explanations sound fragmented.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Provide sentence starters that include subjects and model full sentences before asking students to explain in English; allow Portuguese rehearsal first.

assessment.primary_assessment: Students will be assessed on their responses for the 2.1.7 cool down activity

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Teacher-collected cool-down responses: label parts and answer one short question about a feature.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide labeling options (word bank), allow students to point or circle answers, and permit Portuguese planning notes.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence stems and allow one bilingual cue from the teacher during the task.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a labeled diagram and a one-sentence English explanation; provide a check-list for structure.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Assess correct identification of features first, then language: Level 1-2: correct labels or pointing; Level 3-4: use of a sentence stem to describe a feature; Level 5-6: clear English description with correct terminology.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses only materials referenced by the teacher; no new handouts introduced.

homework.family_connection: At home, students can label parts of a hallway or room (e.g., start, middle, end) with family using Portuguese and English words; ask them to tell one thing they labeled aloud to a family member.

objective.content_objective: Students will explain how to determine the nearest multiple of 10 and the nearest multiple of 100 (orally and in writing).
objective.student_goal: I will explain rounding to tens and hundreds (speaking, writing).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Explain procedures for finding the nearest multiple of 10 and 100 (ELD-MA.2-3.Explain.Reading/Writing) by using bilingual number-line tasks and sentence frames appropriate for WIDA levels 2-4, producing written steps and oral explanations.

anticipatory_set.original_content: Warm-up: Estimation Exploration: What Number Could this Be? (10 minutes)
anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1) Begin with a quick Portuguese prompt: "Este número está mais próximo de dez ou cem?" and elicit gestures/answers. 2) Display a bilingual anchor chart showing tens and hundreds to connect terms before students work in English.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Key unit when identifying nearest multiples of 10.
Used for nearest multiple of 100 comparisons.
Students refer to tens and ones digits when rounding.
Action students perform to estimate numbers.
Describes closeness for both tens and hundreds.
Describes the result of rounding in problem contexts.

sentence_frames.english: This is ___ ten.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Isto é ___ dez.

sentence_frames.english: This is ___ hundred.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Isto é ___ cem.

sentence_frames.english: I look at the ___ digit to decide.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu olho para o dígito ___ para decidir.

sentence_frames.english: If the digit is ___ then I round to ___ because ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Se o dígito for ___ então eu arredondo para ___ porque ___.

sentence_frames.english: I compare distance to the nearest ten and hundred and choose ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu comparo a distância até a dezena e a centena mais próxima e escolho ___.

sentence_frames.english: My estimate is ___ after I round to the nearest ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Minha estimativa é ___ depois de arredondar para a ___.

sentence_frames.english: Evidence shows that rounding to tens or hundreds makes solving the problem easier because ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: As evidências mostram que arredondar para dezenas ou centenas facilita resolver o problema porque ___.

sentence_frames.english: How do you decide whether to round to the nearest ten or the nearest hundred in this problem?
sentence_frames.portuguese: Como você decide arredondar para a dezena mais próxima ou para a centena mais próxima neste problema?

tailored_instruction.original_content: Activity 1: Close to Multiples of 10 (20 minutes). Activity 2: The Nearest Multiples (15 minutes). Students practice naming nearest multiples of 10 and 100 for given three-digit numbers.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Levels 3-4 dominant; stations allow one teacher to support language and the other to focus on procedural math practice.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Class includes emerging bilinguals (Levels 1-2) and majority Levels 3-4; station rotations allow targeted L1 support.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce Portuguese-English digit language and model counting by tens/hundreds.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Pose the estimation warm-up and set success criteria.
phase_plan.details: Quick choral count of tens and hundreds in both languages.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Demonstrate rounding rules at Station A with Portuguese scaffolds for learners needing vocabulary repetition.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Teach rounding shortcuts and model thinking aloud at Station B.
phase_plan.details: Use teacher-created digit cards to show when to round up or down.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support Station A and circulate to prompt use of frames in Portuguese/English.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Oversee Stations B and C focusing on independent practice and quick checks.
phase_plan.details: Students rotate; bilingual teacher records common language errors for closure.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Ask a student to explain in Portuguese then English one example using a frame.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Capture and summarize student explanations for whole-class takeaway.
phase_plan.details: Exit ticket previewed in both languages.

implementation_notes: Create digit cards (0-9) and two bilingual anchor charts (tens, hundreds).
Station signs indicate language supports available at each station.

ell_support.implementation: Use color-coded digit cards; bilingual teacher models 'look at the tens digit' in Portuguese then English; students highlight the deciding digit before rounding.
Provide number-line strips marked at tens and hundreds; bilingual teacher demonstrates placement and language in Portuguese then supports English rehearsal.
Post and practice sentence starters for rounding decisions; encourage students to use one starter orally before writing.

special_needs_support: Provide a 2-choice written response instead of free writing for students needing reduced language production.
Allow extra time for station rotations and use of tactile digit cards for memory support.
materials: bilingual anchor charts for rounding rules

misconceptions.original_content: Students may struggle and confuse rounding to the nearest 100 versus nearest 10.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese speakers may omit subjects, producing English fragments like "Round up because 5" instead of full sentences.
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Use sentence frames with explicit subject prompts ("I round up because ___") and have students practice aloud before writing.

assessment.primary_assessment: [3.3.14 Cool-down](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1CeuNaI75pQ4DVPdCfQElV7iw82FHeWwI/view?usp=drive_link)

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool-down: determine nearest multiple of 10 and 100 for three numbers and justify one choice orally.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide word bank and number-line choices in Portuguese-English; allow pointing or short phrase responses.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frame in English with Portuguese gloss; allow written one-sentence justification.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a short written explanation using connecting words (because, so) and provide minimal prompts.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Mathematical accuracy (correct nearest multiples) prioritized; language scored as: Levels 1-2 key words/phrases, Levels 3-4 complete sentence with reason, Levels 5-6 coherent explanation.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses existing cool-down format and classroom materials only; no extra handouts introduced.

objective.content_objective: Students will complete a culminating writing task demonstrating understanding of culture preservation and include evidence and citations.

sentence_frames.portuguese: Minha estimativa é ___.

sentence_frames.english: This looks reasonable.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Isto parece razoável.

sentence_frames.english: I will check by rounding to ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Vou verificar arredondando para ___.

sentence_frames.english: I think the answer makes sense because my estimate (___) is close to ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu acho que a resposta faz sentido porque minha estimativa (___) está próxima de ___.

sentence_frames.english: After rounding, the sum/difference is ___, so the original answer is ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Depois de arredondar, a soma/diferença é ___, então a resposta original é ___.

sentence_frames.english: I used rounding as a quick check and found ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Usei o arredondamento como verificação rápida e encontrei ___.

sentence_frames.english: Evidence suggests the estimate is reasonable because the rounded computation is within ___ of the original answer.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Evidências sugerem que a estimativa é razoável porque o cálculo arredondado está dentro de ___ da resposta original.

sentence_frames.english: How can rounding help decide if a two-step answer is reasonable?
sentence_frames.portuguese: Como o arredondamento pode ajudar a decidir se a resposta de dois passos é razoável?

tailored_instruction.original_content: Activity 1: Quick Estimates (15 minutes). Activity 2: Solve and Reason (20 minutes). Students use rounding to estimate and then solve two-step word problems involving addition and subtraction to judge reasonableness.

co_teaching_model.rationale: Station Teaching supports quick checks and language scaffolds as students practice estimating and reasoning; appropriate for typical Levels 3-4 distribution.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Mixed proficiency with emphasis on practicing justification language; stations enable targeted feedback.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Model a quick estimate in Portuguese and English and show how to check with rounding.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Introduce the True/False warm-up and set up the two-step tasks.
phase_plan.details: Use a simple example to illustrate 'make sense' reasoning.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Work with small group to practice the English frames for checking reasonableness and rehearse vocabulary.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Model multi-step estimation strategy with a think-aloud.
phase_plan.details: Demonstrate both addition and subtraction checks using rounding.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Support Station A with Portuguese scaffolds and lead partner-talk rehearsals using frames.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate problem solving at Stations B and C and collect formative evidence.
phase_plan.details: Stations: quick estimate practice, two-step problem solving, peer review checks.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Ask a student to present a check in Portuguese then English using the stem.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Summarize checks and note common reasoning errors.
phase_plan.details: Use cool-down item as exit ticket.

implementation_notes: Prepare two-step word-problem prompts and bilingual frame strips.
Have a simple checklist for checks (round, compute, compare) available at each station.

ell_support.implementation: Provide a three-step checklist in Portuguese and English: (1) Round, (2) Compute, (3) Compare. Bilingual teacher models the checklist with one example before students use it independently.

ell_support.strategy_name: Peer Review in L1 then L2
ell_support.implementation: Students first explain their check in Portuguese to a partner, then present the English sentence-frame explanation; bilingual teacher circulates to scaffold transitions.
Provide a side-by-side column: original calculation and rounded calculation; bilingual teacher models comparing columns and uses frames to articulate reasonableness.

special_needs_support: Allow students to show their check with a colored sticky note sequence (round/compute/compare) instead of full written explanation.
Provide sentence starters and allow students to circle prewritten phrases for quick checks.
materials: calculation anchor paper for side-by-side comparisons

misconceptions.original_content: Students may struggle to connect how rounding could be used to assess whether their answers are reasonable.

linguistic_note.note: Portuguese tendency to omit subjects may yield short English fragments when students explain checks (e.g., 'Looks reasonable' instead of 'This looks reasonable').
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Model full-sentence checks and require students to use the sentence frames aloud before writing.

assessment.primary_assessment: [3.3.17 Cool-down](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1nQYeIjFbLvMCB5IEqD5e6TG6mpCuX49p/view?usp=drive_link)

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Cool-down: one estimate and one two-step word problem where students indicate if the solution is reasonable and justify their check.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Allow pointing to rounded calculation and selecting 'reasonable' or 'not reasonable' with a short Portuguese phrase; provide sentence bank.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide a sentence frame and bilingual prompts to write one-sentence justification.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect a two-sentence written justification referencing rounded calculation and comparison.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Math accuracy prioritized; language scoring: key phrase for Levels 1-2, one-sentence justification for Levels 3-4, explicit comparison for Levels 5-6.
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Keeps original cool-down format and uses classroom anchor materials only.

homework.family_connection: Family check: Have the student estimate a grocery total by rounding item prices; the student explains in Portuguese then practices a short English sentence about whether the estimate seems reasonable.

objective.content_objective: Students will learn the steps for resolving conflict and practice communication strategies.
objective.student_goal: I will use steps to resolve conflict with a friend (speaking).
objective.wida_objective: Students will use language to Narrate steps to resolve conflict (ELD-LA.2-3.Narrate.Speaking) by practicing role-play scripts, sentence frames, and bilingual modeling appropriate for WIDA levels 1-4, producing short role-play dialogues that demonstrate understanding of the steps.

anticipatory_set.bilingual_bridge: 1. Teacher introduces a short conflict scenario in Portuguese and asks students how they would feel. 2. Model the resolution steps in Portuguese and repeat in English, connecting emotion words and action verbs across languages.

vocabulary_cognates.relevance_note: Core verb describing the goal of the lesson on conflict resolution.
Key noun students will use to name situations needing resolution.
Used to sequence the resolution process.
Important communication skill practiced during role-plays.
Used to describe emotions during scenarios and practice empathy.
Context for role-play situations practicing resolution steps.

sentence_frames.english: I feel ___ when ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu me sinto ___ quando ___.

sentence_frames.english: Please stop because ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Por favor pare porque ___.

sentence_frames.english: I will try to ___ to help.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu vou tentar ___ para ajudar.

sentence_frames.english: First I will listen, then I will say ___ to show I understand.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Primeiro eu vou ouvir, depois vou dizer ___ para mostrar que entendi.

sentence_frames.english: I feel ___; can we solve this by ___?
sentence_frames.portuguese: Eu me sinto ___; podemos resolver isso por ___?

sentence_frames.english: When we talk, I can hear that you feel ___ because ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Quando conversamos, eu percebo que você se sente ___ porque ___.

sentence_frames.english: Evidence shows that using calm language helps resolve conflict by ___.
sentence_frames.portuguese: Evidências mostram que usar linguagem calma ajuda a resolver conflitos por ___.

sentence_frames.english: How would you recommend resolving this conflict so both friends feel heard?
sentence_frames.portuguese: Como você recomendaria resolver esse conflito para que ambos os amigos se sintam ouvidos?

Differentiated Instruction

co_teaching_model.rationale: Team teaching for social-emotional lessons allows both teachers to model dialogues and coach role-plays in English and Portuguese.
co_teaching_model.wida_context: Levels 3-4 dominant with diverse language needs; team teaching supports demonstration and immediate language coaching.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Introduce emotion vocabulary in Portuguese and English and model a short scenario.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Set up role-play pairs and explain the steps.
phase_plan.details: Model one short dialogue in Portuguese then English; elicit one feeling word from students.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Co-model role-plays, provide immediate feedback in Portuguese and English, and scaffold language during practice.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Facilitate rotations and observe group interactions.
phase_plan.details: Students practice two short role-plays with different partners; teachers circulate and coach.

phase_plan.bilingual_teacher_role: Lead a reflection in Portuguese then English asking students to say one step they learned.
phase_plan.primary_teacher_role: Collect reflections and summarize key language for future reference.
phase_plan.details: Ask 2-3 students to state one step and one feeling word; teachers provide sentence completion feedback.

implementation_notes: Provide simple role-play cards with prompts in both languages.
Use nonverbal cues and visual icons for steps to support comprehension.

ell_support.implementation: Provide sentence stems and model dialogues; bilingual teacher coaches students to rehearse in Portuguese then perform in English or bilingually.
Display a bilingual emotion word bank and icons; encourage students to point to words in Portuguese if they cannot produce English terms immediately.
Train selected students to use frames to guide peers through resolution steps, supporting bilingual exchanges when needed.

special_needs_support: Provide a calm corner and visual script for students who need a reduced stimuli environment.
Allow rehearsals with the bilingual teacher before presenting to peers.

misconceptions.original_content: Vocabulary

linguistic_note.note: Students may confuse false cognates or assume similar words have identical meanings (e.g., 'actual' in English vs 'atual' in Portuguese).
linguistic_note.prevention_tip: Explicitly teach false cognates and check understanding with examples in both languages.

assessment.primary_assessment: “Resolve Conflicts” worksheet

bilingual_overlay.instrument: Worksheet with short responses and role-play assessment; teacher observes role-plays and records language use.

supports_by_level.levels_1_2: Provide phrase banks and allow students to rehearse in Portuguese then perform a short line in English.
supports_by_level.levels_3_4: Provide sentence frames and opportunity for written reflection with bilingual supports.
supports_by_level.levels_5_6: Expect fluent short-role plays and richer explanations of steps with minimal scaffolding.

bilingual_overlay.scoring_lens: Assess ability to state a step, use emotion vocabulary, and apply a simple resolution strategy; note language production level (phrase, sentence, fluent).
bilingual_overlay.constraints_honored: Uses existing worksheet and classroom materials; no new materials introduced.

homework.family_connection: Families can role-play a short conflict scenario at home using one English and one Portuguese sentence from the emotion word bank; students report back one sentence learned.
